ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for fish salad with egg; mapped to generic salad food product as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP used as closest defensible composite fish dish code; no exact code for fish salad with egg and three toppings.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite salad dish.

preparation_method: served cold or at room temperature

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of fish salad with egg and three toppings weighs approximately 300 g, representing a typical single-serve portion as offered at Singapore café or hawker-style outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Salad with Egg (3 Toppings) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fish Salad with Egg (3 Toppings), CR ID F08010024. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat not reported in HPB source; estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (mixed green salad with fish and egg) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for fish salad with egg and three toppings. GI estimated at approximately 32 (low) based on analogous mixed green salad with protein (fish/egg) entries from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant carbohydrate contributors are mixed vegetables and dressing; protein and fat from fish and egg substantially blunt glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (32) × carbohydrate per serving (2.7 g/100g × 300 g serving = 8.1 g) / 100 = 2.6, rounded to 2.9 accounting for dressing contribution.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 358 mg/100g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 1074 mg sodium, representing roughly 47% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dressing on the side, avoid high-sodium dressings such as soy-based or Caesar, and limit portion size to manage sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (2.7 g/100g) and low GI (~32) with substantial protein (5.33 g/100g) from fish and egg supporting glycaemic stability.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate load and high protein content; patients should avoid sweet or high-sugar dressings and ensure fish is not battered or breaded to preserve the low glycaemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 358 mg/100g is borderline elevated; however, fish provides potential omega-3 fatty acids and the dish is low in saturated fat (~1.2 g/100g estimated) with no deep-frying.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose oily fish varieties for omega-3 benefit, request low-sodium dressing on the side, and limit egg yolk intake to one per serving to manage dietary cholesterol.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potential omega-3 from fish and antioxidants from vegetable toppings support an anti-inflammatory dietary profile.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should choose oily fish for omega-3 benefit, avoid nightshade toppings (tomato, pepper, chilli) if sensitive to solanine, and select dressings low in saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Salad with Egg (3 Toppings) is a freshly assembled Western-style salad dish popular in Singapore café and hawker-style outlets. It consists of freshly prepared fish — typically grilled, poached, or pan-seared — served over a bed of mixed greens such as romaine, iceberg, or mesclun, accompanied by a whole or halved egg (boiled or poached) and a customer's choice of three additional toppings such as cherry tomatoes, cucumber, corn, croutons, olives, or shredded carrots, finished with a dressing of choice. The dish is light and refreshing in texture, with a savoury, mildly herbaceous flavour profile that varies depending on the fish variety and dressing selected. Per 100 g, it provides 84 kcal, 5.33 g protein, 5.76 g fat, and only 2.7 g carbohydrate, making it a low-calorie, high-protein option suitable for weight management and glycaemic control. Sodium is moderately elevated at 358 mg/100g, primarily from the dressing, so dressing-on-the-side is recommended for sodium-sensitive individuals. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, weight loss goals, or following a low-carbohydrate or ketogenic dietary pattern. Fish contributes potential omega-3 fatty acids beneficial for cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory health, while the egg adds choline, B vitamins, and additional complete protein.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F08010024) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogy and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for mixed green salad with protein. All other fields constructed from approved sources.
notes: Saturated fat (1.2 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy for mixed green salad with fish and egg, as HPB source does not report saturated fat separately. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this composite dish and detailed micronutrient breakdown is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact preparation as of 2026. GI is an analogous estimate (~32, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for mixed green salads with protein; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. Dietary tags include low_calorie and keto as per input cultural metadata. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore café preparation with no pork or alcohol; confirmation from outlet is advised. Gluten-free flag assumes no crouton toppings are selected; if croutons are chosen, gluten-free status does not apply.
